zh.lifeSummary: 當Margaret Webber於1761年出生在美國緬因州約克威爾斯時，她的父親Edmund Webber Jr23歲，母親Dorothy Stewart24歲。她於1780年1月11日與Samuel Morrison在美國緬因州約克威爾斯結婚。他們至少是10個兒子和6個女兒的父母。她去世於1831年11月25日在美國緬因州阿盧斯圖可劍橋大學默里 利默里克享年70歲，埋葬在美國緬因州阿盧斯圖可卡利布曼恩。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Margaret Webber于1761年出生在美国缅因州約克威爾斯时，她的父亲Edmund Webber Jr23岁，母亲Dorothy Stewart24岁。她于1780年1月11日与Samuel Morrison在美国缅因州約克威爾斯结婚。他们至少是10个儿子和6个女儿的父母。她去世于1831年11月25日在美国缅因州阿盧斯圖可劍橋大學默里 利默里克享年70岁，埋葬在美国缅因州阿盧斯圖可卡利布曼恩。

nameMeanings.name: Webber
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (Devon and Somerset): occupational name for a weaver, from Middle English webber, an agent derivative of Webb .
Americanized form of German and Jewish (Ashkenazic)   Weber   ‘weaver’.

variants.name: Webb
meanings: English: occupational name for a weaver, from early Middle English webbe (Old English webba (masculine) or webbe (feminine), probably used of both male and female weavers). This word survived into Middle English long enough to give rise to the surname, but was already obsolescent as an agent noun; hence the secondary forms with the agent suffixes -er and -ster (see Webster , Webber and compare Weaver ).
Americanized form of various like-sounding Jewish (Ashkenazic) surnames, cognates of 1, including Weber and Weberman.
History: Richard Webb, a Lowland Scot, was an admitted freeman of Boston in 1632, and in 1635 was one of the first settlers of Hartford, CT.

nameMeanings.name: Margaret
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: An extremely common given name from the Middle Ages onwards, derived via Old French Marguerite and Latin Margarita from Greek Margarītēs, from margaron ‘pearl’, a word ultimately of Hebrew origin. The name was always understood to mean ‘pearl’ throughout the Middle Ages. The first St Margaret was martyred at Antioch in Pisidia during the persecution instigated by the Emperor Diocletian in the early 4th century. However, there is some doubt about her name, as the same saint is venerated in the Orthodox Church as Marina . There were several other saintly bearers of the name, including St Margaret of Scotland ( d. 1093 ), wife of King Malcolm Canmore and daughter of Edmund Ironside of England. It was also the name of the wife of Henry VI of England, Margaret of Anjou ( 1430–82 ), and of Margaret Tudor ( 1489–1541 ), sister of Henry VIII , who married James IV of Scotland and ruled as regent there after his death. See also Margery , Marjorie .

variants.name: Margareta
meanings: From the Latin form of Greek Margarītēs. See Margaret .

variants.name: Margaretta
meanings: An extremely common given name from the Middle Ages onwards, derived via Old French Marguerite and Latin Margarita from Greek Margarītēs, from margaron ‘pearl’, a word ultimately of Hebrew origin. The name was always understood to mean ‘pearl’ throughout the Middle Ages. The first St Margaret was martyred at Antioch in Pisidia during the persecution instigated by the Emperor Diocletian in the early 4th century. However, there is some doubt about her name, as the same saint is venerated in the Orthodox Church as Marina . There were several other saintly bearers of the name, including St Margaret of Scotland ( d. 1093 ), wife of King Malcolm Canmore and daughter of Edmund Ironside of England. It was also the name of the wife of Henry VI of England, Margaret of Anjou ( 1430–82 ), and of Margaret Tudor ( 1489–1541 ), sister of Henry VIII , who married James IV of Scotland and ruled as regent there after his death. See also Margery , Marjorie .

historicEvents.description: Historical Boundaries: 1775: Lincoln, Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America 1776: Lincoln, Massachusetts, United States 1790: Washington, Massachusetts, United States 1820, Washington, Maine, United States 1839: Aroostook, Maine, United States
Thomas Jefferson's American Declaration of Independence endorsed by Congress. Colonies declare independence.

historicEvents.title: The First Constitution
historicEvents.description: Serving the newly created United States of America as the first constitution, the Articles of Confederation were an agreement among the 13 original states preserving the independence and sovereignty of the states. But with a limited central government, the Constitutional Convention came together to replace the Articles of Confederation with a more established Constitution and central government on where the states can be represented and voice their concerns and comments to build up the nation.

historicEvents.title: A Free America
historicEvents.description: The Revolutionary War ended with the signing of the Treaty of Paris which gave the new nation boundries on which they could expand and trade with other countries without any problems.

historicEvents.title: Movement to Washington D.C.
historicEvents.description: While the growth of the new nation was exponential, the United States didn’t have permanent location to house the Government. The First capital was temporary in New York City but by the second term of George Washington the Capital moved to Philadelphia for the following 10 years. Ultimately during the Presidency of John Adams, the Capital found a permanent home in the District of Columbia.

historicEvents.title: Whitehead Light
historicEvents.description: In 1804, President Thomas Jefferson authorized the creation of a light station on Whitehead Island. The light house went into service by 1807. It is the third-oldest light house in Maine. Whitehead Light still exists as the private property of Pine Island Camp, a non-profit organization.

historicEvents.title: Panic! of 1819
historicEvents.description: With the Aftermath of the Napoleonic Wars the global market for trade was down. During this time, America had its first financial crisis and it lasted for only two years.
Historical Boundaries: 1820: Washington, Massachusetts, United States: 1820: Washington, Maine, United States: 1839: Aroostook, Maine, United States

en.lifeSummary: When Margaret Webber was born in 1761, in Wells, York, Maine, United States, her father, Edmund Webber Jr, was 23 and her mother, Dorothy Stewart, was 24. She married Samuel Morrison on 11 January 1780, in Wells, York, Maine, United States. They were the parents of at least 10 sons and 6 daughters. She died on 25 November 1831, in New Limerick, Aroostook, Maine, United States, at the age of 70, and was buried in Maine, Caribou, Aroostook, Maine, United States.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Margaret Webber est née en 1761, à Wells, York, Maine, États-Unis, son père, Edmund Webber Jr, avait 23 ans et sa mère, Dorothy Stewart, avait 24 ans. Elle a épousé Samuel Morrison le 11 janvier 1780, à Wells, York, Maine, États-Unis. Ils ont eu au moins 10 fils et 6 filles. Elle est décédée le 25 novembre 1831, à New Limerick, Aroostook, Maine, États-Unis, à l’âge de 70 ans, et a été enterrée à Maine, Caribou, Aroostook, Maine, États-Unis.

ja.lifeSummary: Margaret Webberが1761年にアメリカ合衆国メイン州ヨークウエルズで生まれた時，父Edmund Webber Jrは23歳，母Dorothy Stewartは24歳であった。1780年1月11日にアメリカ合衆国メイン州ヨークウエルズでSamuel Morrisonと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも10人の息子と6人の娘の親であった。彼女は1831年11月25日にアメリカ合衆国メイン州アルーストオクニュー リメリックで70歳で死去し，アメリカ合衆国メイン州アルーストオクカーイボウメインにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Margaret Webber(이)가 1761년 미국 메인 주 요르크 웰스에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Edmund Webber Jr(은)는 23세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Dorothy Stewart(은)는 24세였다. 그녀는 1780년 1월 11일 미국 메인 주 요르크 웰스에서 Samuel Morrison(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 열 명의 아들과 여섯 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1831년 11월 25일 미국 메인 주 아루스투크 뉴 라임릭에서 70세의 나이로 사망했으며 미국 메인 주 아루스투크 카리보우 맹에 묻혔다.

pt.New Limerick, Aroostook, Maine, United S: New Limerick, Aroostook, Maine, Estados Unidos da América
pt.lifeSummary: Quando Margaret Webber nasceu em 1761, em Wells, York, Maine, Estados Unidos da América, seu pai, Edmund Webber Jr, tinha 23 anos e sua mãe, Dorothy Stewart, tinha 24 anos. Ela casou-se com Samuel Morrison em 11 de janeiro de 1780, em Wells, York, Maine, Estados Unidos da América. Eles tiveram pelo menos 10 filhos e 6 filhas. Ela faleceu em 25 de novembro de 1831, em New Limerick, Aroostook, Maine, Estados Unidos da América, com 70 anos, e foi sepultada em Maine, Caribou, Aroostook, Maine, Estados Unidos da América.

ru.Lineus,Aroostook,Maine: меин, карибоу, арустук, Мэн, Соединенные штаты
ru.New Limerick, Aroostook, Maine, United S: ню лимирикк, арустук, Мэн, Соединенные штаты
ru.lifeSummary: Когда Margaret Webber родилась в 1761 г. в местности веллс, ерк, Мэн, Соединенные штаты, её отцу по имени Edmund Webber Jr было 23 года, а матери, которую звали Dorothy Stewart, 24. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Samuel Morrison 11 января 1780 г. в местности веллс, ерк, Мэн, Соединенные штаты. У них было по крайней мере 10 сыновей и 6 дочерей. Она умерла 25 ноября 1831 г. в местности ню лимирикк, арустук, Мэн, Соединенные штаты в возрасте 70 лет и была похоронена в местности меин, карибоу, арустук, Мэн, Соединенные штаты.

es.New Limerick, Aroostook, Maine, United S: New Limerick, Aroostook, Maine, Estados Unidos
es.lifeSummary: Cuando Margaret Webber nació en 1761, en Wells, York, Maine, Estados Unidos, su papá, Edmund Webber Jr, tenía 23 años y su mamá, Dorothy Stewart, tenía 24 años. Se casó con Samuel Morrison el 11 de enero de 1780, en Wells, York, Maine, Estados Unidos. Fueron padres de por lo menos 10 hijos y 6 hijas. Murió el 25 de noviembre de 1831, en New Limerick, Aroostook, Maine, Estados Unidos, a la edad de 70 años, y fue sepultada en Maine, Caribou, Aroostook, Maine, Estados Unidos.
